_portable_note: Portable Brayan/Darwin cron job definitions. Volatile run state was reset by scripts/sync-brayan-personalization.py. Secrets are not included.

jobs.name: darwin-job-tailoring-agent
hermes-upstream-rebase-ci
jobs.prompt: You are Darwin running Brayan's Hermes upstream rebase CI exception handler. Read and follow `~/.hermes/agents/hermes-upstream-rebase-ci/prompt-template.md`; stable behavior lives in `~/.hermes/skills/hermes-upstream-rebase-ci-agent/SKILL.md`. The pre-run script is primary and should wake this prompt only on failure/action-needed. Do not schedule or modify cron jobs from this run.

jobs.name: max-planck-cs-internship-2027-prep-reminder
jobs.prompt: Remind Brayan to start the P0 Max Planck CS Research Internships 2027 preparation project. Context: vault note [[opportunities/2026-04-28-max-planck-cs-research-internships-2027/opportunity]]. Summer-start internships are due November 1 of the previous year. Recommended actions: choose target start window, shortlist MPI faculty/research groups, audit transcript/CV/research-portfolio gaps, identify recommender, and decide whether to build a small aligned proof-of-work project.

jobs.name: max-planck-cs-internship-2027-final-application-reminder
jobs.prompt: Remind Brayan that the Max Planck CS Research Internships summer-cycle deadline is November 1 of the previous year. Context: vault note [[opportunities/2026-04-28-max-planck-cs-research-internships-2027/opportunity]]. Final checklist: CV, transcripts, at least one reference contact with prior permission, optional motivation letter, target faculty/research groups, intended internship period, and post-registration form inspection.

jobs.name: fermi-summer-school-2027-cycle-check
jobs.prompt: Remind Brayan to check whether the NASA/Fermi Summer School 2027 page/application is live. Context: vault note [[opportunities/2026-04-28-fermi-summer-school-2027-watch/opportunity]]. The 2026 deadline was February 6 and early applications were favored, especially for applicants needing a US visa. Check the new deadline, eligibility, fee/support, and whether Brayan has a credible physics/data-analysis statement or project before applying.

jobs.name: erasmus-scholarship-shortlist-reminder
jobs.prompt: Reminder/task for Darwin: follow up on Brayan's Erasmus+ scholarship application interest. Use the personal-vault notes at ~/personal_vault/opportunities/2026-04-28-erasmus-scholarship-international-mobility-watch/opportunity.md and ~/personal_vault/opportunities/2026-04-28-erasmus-scholarship-international-mobility-watch/application/application-strategy.md. Deliver a concise reminder to Brayan to build a 10-20 Erasmus/Erasmus Mundus programme shortlist and collect document blockers (transcripts, English proof, passport, recommendations). If web tools are available, optionally include 3-5 promising programme-search starting points, but do not fabricate deadlines.

jobs.name: erasmus-icetex-deadline-window-check
jobs.prompt: Reminder/task for Darwin: start the likely Erasmus/Erasmus Mundus and ICETEX scholarship deadline window check for Brayan. Use ~/personal_vault/opportunities/2026-04-28-erasmus-scholarship-international-mobility-watch/opportunity.md, ~/personal_vault/opportunities/2026-04-28-erasmus-scholarship-international-mobility-watch/application/application-strategy.md, and ~/personal_vault/opportunities/2026-04-28-icetex-international-scholarships-search-watch/opportunity.md. Check current public pages if possible, summarize next deadlines/requisites if verifiable, and tell Brayan what to decide/prep next. Do not submit applications.

jobs.name: vault-structure-auditor
jobs.prompt:
You are Darwin running Brayan's weekly Vault v2 structure auditor. Read and follow `~/.hermes/agents/vault-structure-auditor/prompt-template.md` and the loaded `personal-vault-ops` + `vault-structure-auditor-agent` skills.

A deterministic pre-run script (`vault_structure_audit.py`) has already executed and injected JSON context plus a report path. Treat that script output as the first-pass inventory, not the whole audit.

Your task:
1. Read the generated audit report under `~/personal_vault/_meta/audits/`.
2. Use the script findings for counts, broken links, frontmatter errors, retired-path references, semantic location issues, and git status.
3. Independently perform a systematic semantic organization pass: inspect suspicious filenames/titles and representative notes from every top-level folder; verify passive tool/resource catalogs live under `references/`; verify `inbox/` has no durable `_captures` store; verify true projects use `projects/<slug>/README.md`; verify opportunities use `opportunities/<slug>/opportunity.md` plus optional `application/` materials; verify workflows/guides/architecture remain under `_meta/`.
4. If there are issues, send a concise Telegram report with exact patch proposal groups, affected paths, risk level, and recommended next action.
5. Do not move/delete/archive notes or apply patches in this cron run. This weekly job is report-only unless Brayan later approves fixes interactively.
6. Return `[SILENT]` if both the deterministic script and your semantic pass find no meaningful issue.

jobs.name: Modal credits utilization review until expiry
jobs.prompt: You are Darwin running a daily reminder about Brayan's temporary Modal-credit directive. Context: Brayan has USD $500 of Modal credits expiring on 2026-05-14; unused credits are lost. This is not a project. It is a temporary short-term directive to consider Modal for OTHER useful AI/physics/coding/research work where bounded compute helps. Do not create a project just to spend credits. Do not change or reinterpret any existing project-specific budget caps. Do not spend money or launch GPU jobs yourself in this reminder job. Read /home/brayan/personal_vault/decisions/pending.md, /home/brayan/personal_vault/_meta/dashboards/project-dashboard.md, /home/brayan/personal_vault/opportunities/dashboard.md if present, and /home/brayan/personal_vault/raw/notes/2026-04-30-modal-credits-expiring-capture.md. Check current Modal billing with `modal billing report --for today --json` if Modal CLI is available. Produce a concise reminder: days left until 2026-05-14, observed spend if available, 1-3 concrete existing projects/opportunities where Modal could help soon, and any decision needed. Keep it practical and budget-aware.
